description: Relationship type definitions for the Synapse steel-plant knowledge graph. Each entry defines one Neo4j relationship type and the valid (from_label -> to_label) pairs it may connect. 'implementation' is 'edge' for real graph relationships, or 'property' for cases the spec allows modeling as a property instead of a dedicated edge.

properties.name: date
properties.notes: Date the coil was produced on this equipment

properties.name: quantity_used
properties.notes: Quantity of raw material consumed for this coil

properties.name: test_date
properties.notes: Date the quality test was performed

relationships.notes: Only QualityTests that actually breached their standard -- i.e. that have a linked Deviation (644). Tests performed with no resulting deviation use TESTED_AGAINST instead. FAILED_STANDARD + TESTED_AGAINST together cover all 1941 QualityTests.

A QualityTest evaluated against a Standard that did NOT breach it (no linked Deviation). Complement of FAILED_STANDARD; the two are mutually exclusive and jointly exhaustive over all QualityTests, so compliance rate = FAILED_STANDARD / (FAILED_STANDARD + TESTED_AGAINST) and every test still traces back to a standard.

properties.name: timestamp
properties.notes: Date and time the failure occurred

relationships.notes: Represented today as the RawMaterial.supplier_id property rather than a dedicated edge. If a Vendor node label is introduced later, promote this to a real edge: (RawMaterial)-[:SUPPLIED_BY]->(Vendor).

Represented today as the Equipment.location property rather than a dedicated edge. If a Warehouse/Location node label is introduced later, promote this to a real edge: (Equipment)-[:LOCATED_AT]->(Location).

properties.name: version_number
properties.notes: Version number of the revision chain; edge points from the newer version to the prior version it supersedes
